---
title: "Lab 3 Leaderboard — AY26 Q3"
subtitle: "USV Waypoint Mission Challenge — Day 1 (2026-06-03)"
---

[← Back to Lab 3 overview](index.qmd)

## Current standings (unverified)

Best course time per team on challenge day 1 (2026-06-03). Times are
the autopilot's own WP1-to-WP7 elapsed, as logged in the `MSG`
"Reached waypoint #N" events — see [Scoring notes](#scoring-notes)
below.

| Rank | Team | Best time | UTC time of run | Log |
|:---:|---|---:|:---:|:---:|
| 1 | cyborgs | 01:15 (75.2 s) | 2026-06-03 18:41:27 | `00000051.BIN` |
| 2 | team3 | 01:43 (103.4 s) | 2026-06-03 18:26:57 | `00000039.BIN` |
| 3 | blackpearl | 03:26 (205.8 s) | 2026-06-03 18:28:14 | `00000049.BIN` |
|  | *Instructor benchmark* | *03:13 (193.4 s)* | *2026-05-29 (prototype day)* | — |



## Per-team breakdown

Parameter-change summaries are general descriptions, not exact details. The point is to convey what each team tuned relative to the lab baseline `.param`.

### cyborgs

Seven complete-course runs on challenge day, with a clear improvement
arc as they tuned.

| # | Time | UTC | Log |
|:-:|---:|:--|:--|
| 1 | 03:15 (195.2 s) | 2026-06-03 18:31:10 | `00000048.BIN` |
| 2 | 01:28 (87.8 s)  | 2026-06-03 18:37:24 | `00000049.BIN` |
| 3 | 01:15 (75.2 s)  | 2026-06-03 18:41:27 | `00000051.BIN` |
| 4 | 01:19 (79.2 s)  | 2026-06-03 18:43:57 | `00000052.BIN` |
| 5 | 01:15 (75.2 s)  | 2026-06-03 18:49:04 | `00000053.BIN` |
| 6 | 01:22 (81.6 s)  | 2026-06-03 18:54:05 | `00000057.BIN` |
| 7 | 01:20 (79.5 s)  | 2026-06-03 18:58:56 | `00000058.BIN` |

Tuning direction relative to baseline:

- Applied the standard fix to the stick-to-rate scaling.
- Disabled the speed-axis rate limiter.
- Raised the waypoint cruise speed modestly (a small multiple of baseline).
- Zeroed out the speed-loop feedforward gain (relying entirely on PID closure).
- Raised the pivot-turn threshold so moderate course-changes are handled with continuous steering instead of stop-and-pivot.

### team3

Four complete-course runs, all clustered tightly in the 103-108 s range.

| # | Time | UTC | Log |
|:-:|---:|:--|:--|
| 1 | 01:48 (108.0 s) | 2026-06-03 18:20:43 | `00000037.BIN` |
| 2 | 01:43 (103.4 s) | 2026-06-03 18:26:57 | `00000039.BIN` |
| 3 | 01:45 (104.5 s) | 2026-06-03 18:34:12 | `00000041.BIN` |
| 4 | 01:44 (104.3 s) | 2026-06-03 18:42:15 | `00000043.BIN` |

Tuning direction relative to baseline:

- Applied the standard stick-to-rate fix.
- Disabled the speed-axis rate limiter.
- Pushed both the cruise-speed scaling and the waypoint cruise speed to very high values — well above the boat's nominal cruise.
- Maxed out the throttle baseline bias.
- Disabled the pivot-turn feature entirely.
- Slashed the steering proportional and integral gains, sharply raised the steering feedforward, and added a derivative term.
- Switched the primary heading reference between compass instances.

team3 chose to commit to high-speed transit and accept that the boat would overshoot every waypoint. The autopilot was happy with that — it advanced through the mission using perpendicular-crossing detection (the "finish-line" criterion documented under [ArduRover → Tuning Navigation](https://ardupilot.org/rover/docs/rover-tuning-navigation.html); see also the discussion in [ArduPilot issue #23457](https://github.com/ArduPilot/ardupilot/issues/23457) about how WP_RADIUS is effectively bypassed at speed) rather than waiting for the boat to dip inside the strict acceptance radius.


That they didn't win on raw time is a separate observation. Whatever cyborgs was doing at the steering layer let them carry similar (or higher) average speed through the course geometry with cleaner traces through the corners — but neither configuration "broke" the autopilot.

### blackpearl

One complete-course run.

| # | Time | UTC | Log |
|:-:|---:|:--|:--|
| 1 | 03:26 (205.8 s) | 2026-06-03 18:28:14 | `00000049.BIN` |

Tuning direction relative to baseline:

- Applied the standard stick-to-rate fix.
- Disabled the speed-axis rate limiter.
- Roughly doubled the cruise-speed scaling and significantly raised the waypoint cruise speed.
- Added a constant throttle baseline bias on top of the per-step feedforward.
- Heavily retuned the speed-loop PID — substantially more aggressive across proportional, integral, derivative, and feedforward.
- Reduced the steering PID gains while raising the steering feedforward.
- Re-enabled the R/C-loss failsafe (the procedure recommends turning this off for the challenge course).

## Scoring notes

A complete-course run is one where the autopilot's `MSG` log contains
`"Reached waypoint #N"` for N = 1 .. 7 in that order. This is the
autopilot's own mission-advancement signal, which uses *both* of:

- The boat entering the waypoint's acceptance radius (`WP_RADIUS`).
- The boat crossing a "finish line" — a line through the waypoint, perpendicular to the path segment leading to it. Once the boat is on the far side of that line, the autopilot treats the waypoint as reached even if the boat never came inside `WP_RADIUS`.

The second condition is the surprising one and matters at speed. At the AY26 Q3 course geometry, a boat moving above ~3 m/s can legitimately overshoot a waypoint without ever dipping inside a 1 m radius. The autopilot still advances the mission, the `MSG` log still records "Reached waypoint #N", and on the water the team sees a clean lap — but a scoring script that only checks `WP_RADIUS` entry will undercount finishes. This is exactly what happened to team3's results before the scoring script was corrected.

The behavior is documented (loosely) in the [ArduRover → Tuning Navigation](https://ardupilot.org/rover/docs/rover-tuning-navigation.html) page (look for `WP_OVERSHOOT` and the "finish line" discussion). [ArduPilot issue #23457](https://github.com/ArduPilot/ardupilot/issues/23457) — "AR_WPNav: Rover 4.3+ does not honor WP_RADIUS" — has more candid detail on how the perpendicular check has effectively superseded the radius check since the 4.3 navigation rewrite.

## Notes

- Per-team parameter summaries are drawn from a representative challenge-day log per team; the underlying logs are in `/data/ME2801_USV/2026_06_03_challengeday1/logs/` (instructor-only access).
- Day 2 results, when available, will be appended in a follow-on table above.
